---
title: "Open hours"
description: "Set the hours a menu is available. Use it for breakfast, lunch, and dinner menus, or any other window."
sidebarTitle: "Open hours"
icon: "clock"
---

Each menu can have its own schedule — the hours when diners should see it.
Open hours are also what powers the "Out until next opening" availability
mode for out-of-stock items.

## Where to set it

In the menu editor, swipe to the **Schedule** tab. You'll see a seven-day
grid, one column per day of the week.

## Adding windows

<Steps>
  <Step title="Pick a day">
    Tap a day to edit its windows.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Add a time range">
    Set the start and end times — for example, **11:30 → 14:30** for
    lunch. You can add more than one window per day. Lunch and dinner? Add
    two windows.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Copy to other days">
    Tap **Copy** on a configured day, then **Paste** on the next day. Fast
    way to clone Monday's hours to Tuesday.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Save">
    Tap **Save** in the toolbar. Nothing is saved until you do.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Presets and shortcuts

- **24-hour preset** — tap to set a day to open all day (00:00 → 24:00).
- **Clear day** — tap to remove every window for a day (menu is closed on
  that day).
- **Overlapping windows** are merged automatically. If you enter *11:00 →
  13:00* and *12:30 → 15:00*, the editor saves a single *11:00 → 15:00*
  window.

## Timezone

All times use your venue's timezone (set in **Settings**). If your venue
is in Tehran and you set lunch to *12:00*, diners in any timezone see the
menu open at 12:00 local time in Tehran.

## How diners experience it

Open hours power two things today:

1. **Default menu selection** — when you have multiple menus, Rumizi picks
   the menu whose current window is open for the next scan of your QR
   code.
2. **"Out until next opening"** — when you mark an item out of stock and
   pick this mode, Rumizi brings it back at the start of the next
   scheduled window.

<Note>
Open hours control which menu is *shown first*, not whether diners can
scan your QR code. The menu is always reachable at your public URL — open
hours just guide which menu is the default at a given time.
</Note>
